meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Finish Your AI App Today
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn professional project management for student developers using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS app.
content.answer_block: You fail to finish projects because you lack a scope-constrained architecture. By adopting an iterative, milestone-based workflow using Antigravity for planning and Claude Code for execution, you transform overwhelming ideas into a series of shippable, bite-sized features. Stop building 'everything' and start building a functional, high-impact MVP.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions. You start with a burst of adrenaline, scaffolding a complex AI architecture, only to hit the 'middle-of-the-project' wall where complexity suffocates your progress. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a tactical deployment strategy.

Professional engineering isn't about writing the most code; it’s about managing the cognitive load of the system. By leveraging modern AI tooling like Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to maintain velocity, you can turn your unfinished side projects into a portfolio that commands attention. Let's rebuild your workflow from the ground up.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Narrow your scope to a single, high-value feature. Avoid 'feature creep' by documenting only what is strictly necessary to solve the core user problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project idea and break it down into the absolute minimum viable feature set. Exclude all non-essential UI/UX polish for the first release.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a README focusing strictly on the MVP scope defined in the planning phase.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, define how data flows. Using Antigravity to map your database schema prevents costly refactoring later in the development cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a relational database schema for this AI app. Include tables for users, prompts, and output history, optimized for PostgreSQL.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create the schema.prisma file based on the provided Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Never write boilerplate by hand. Use Claude Code to scaffold your backend services and API routes based on your predefined schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a clean, modular directory structure for a Next.js AI application that follows industry best practices for scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Initialize the project structure and install dependencies as specified by the architecture plan.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Sprints
steps.description: Break development into 2-hour sprints. Focus on one functionality at a time to maintain momentum and see tangible progress daily.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Decompose the current feature into small, manageable tasks that can be completed in under 90 minutes of focused coding.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Implement the authentication service using the modular structure defined in step 3.'

steps.title: Rigorous Error Handling & Testing
steps.description: A project isn't finished until it's resilient. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your edge cases, ensuring the app doesn't break in production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the most likely failure points in this AI integration and suggest robust error handling strategies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate unit tests for the main API routes and implement global error boundary handling.'

pro_tips: Always start your day by deleting one non-essential feature from your roadmap to reduce technical debt.
Use Claude Code to write your commit messages; it forces you to summarize the 'why' behind every code change.
If you feel stuck for more than 30 minutes, switch to a different component of the app to keep your brain fresh.
Treat your README as a living document; update it after every session to track your progress visually.
Prioritize 'shippable' states over 'perfect' code; you can always refactor once the feature is live.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel or Railway, gather feedback from three real users, and iterate based on their specific pain points rather than adding new features.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical roadmap and the mental framework to ship your first AI app. Stop planning and start executing. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
